tool.name: IQ-TREE 3
tool.description: Maximum-likelihood phylogenetic inference with model selection
tool.command:
ln -sf '$alignment' 'aln.fa' &&
## UFBoot (-B) hangs on alignments with fewer than 4 distinct sequences. ## Count distinct sequences (record-aware: join each record onto one line, ## strip the header, then dedup) and drop -B below the threshold. n_unique=\$(awk '/^>/{if(seq!="")print seq; seq=""; next}{seq=seq \$0}END{if(seq!="")print seq}' 'aln.fa' | sort -u | wc -l) &&
iqtree3 -s 'aln.fa' -m MFP #if $bootstrap \$( [ "\$n_unique" -ge 4 ] && echo "-B 1000" ) #end if -T \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-2} --prefix out

param.name: alignment
param.label: Sequence alignment
param.help: A codon or protein multiple-sequence alignment in aligned FASTA format.

param.name: bootstrap
param.label: Ultrafast bootstrap (-B 1000)
param.help: Run 1000 ultrafast bootstrap replicates. Automatically skipped when the alignment has fewer than 4 distinct sequences (UFBoot hangs below this threshold).

tool.help:
**IQ-TREE 3** — maximum-likelihood phylogenetic inference.
IQ-TREE infers a maximum-likelihood phylogenetic tree from a multiple sequence alignment. With `-m MFP` (ModelFinder Plus) it first selects the best-fit substitution model, then searches for the ML tree under that model and, optionally, assesses branch support with the ultrafast bootstrap (UFBoot).
This wrapper runs the canonical pangenome per-gene tree command:
``` iqtree3 -s aln.fa -m MFP -B 1000 -T ${GALAXY_SLOTS:-2} --prefix out ```
-----
**Input**
- `Sequence alignment` — a codon or protein multiple-sequence alignment in aligned FASTA format.
**Outputs**
- `ML tree (Newick)` — the inferred maximum-likelihood tree (`out.treefile`). - `Report` — the IQ-TREE run report with the selected model and log-likelihood (`out.iqtree`).
-----
**Ultrafast bootstrap and small alignments**
UFBoot (`-B 1000`) hangs on alignments with fewer than four distinct sequences. The wrapper counts distinct sequences in the alignment and automatically drops `-B` below that threshold, so the run still completes and emits a tree even for 2- or 3-sequence inputs.
